factors.summary: Demand is accelerating, driven by tonnage growth and market share gains.

evidence.text: Revenue increased 12.2% and was driven by significant tonnage growth attributable in part to further market share gains.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Demand is accelerating due to tonnage growth and market share gains.

factors.summary: The company is successfully holding prices, with revenue per hundredweight increasing despite declining fuel surcharges.

evidence.text: LTL revenue per hundredweight increased 0.4% to $18.08 from $18.00 in the prior-year quarter, despite the significant decline in fuel surcharges.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue per hundredweight increased despite declining fuel surcharges.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments through premium services and yield management.

evidence.text: We believe the increase in tonnage continues to be driven by further market share gains as the value of our premium service resonates with our customers.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments through premium services.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, with a 12.2% increase in revenue and a 36.3% increase in net income.

evidence.text: Revenue increased 12.2% and was driven by significant tonnage growth attributable in part to further market share gains. This tonnage growth contributed to greater density within our operations, and when combined with our yield management initiatives, led to improved profitability.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is accelerating with significant increases in revenue and net income.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, driven by real demand rather than price increases.

evidence.text: LTL shipments (in thousands) increased 13.5%, and LTL tons (in thousands) increased 11.4%. LTL revenue per shipment decreased 1.4%, indicating that growth is driven by volume rather than price.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are growing driven by real demand rather than price increases.

factors.summary: The company is gaining market share, particularly in premium services, which is driving growth.

evidence.text: We believe the increase in tonnage continues to be driven by further market share gains as the value of our premium service resonates with our customers.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is gaining market share, particularly in premium services.

factors.summary: Input costs, particularly fuel, are declining, which is easing cost pressures.

evidence.text: Fuel surcharge revenue decreased to 11.5% of our revenue for the first quarter of 2015 from 16.2% for the same period of 2014, primarily due to a decrease in the average price per gallon of diesel fuel between the periods compared.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Declining fuel costs are easing cost pressures.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing due to higher wages and benefits, creating margin pressure.

evidence.text: Salaries, wages and benefits increased $57.3 million, or 18.4% in the first quarter of 2015 due to a $45.0 million increase in the costs for salaries and wages and a $12.3 million increase in benefit costs.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are increasing due to higher wages and benefits, creating margin pressure.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are expanding due to improved pricing power and cost management.

evidence.text: Our operating ratio improved 200 basis points to 85.1%, which contributed to a 36.3% increase in net income and a 37.7% increase in earnings per diluted share as compared to the prior-year period.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: The summary indicates significant improvement in margins due to pricing power and cost management.

factors.summary: Cost reduction initiatives, such as fuel efficiency and reduced purchased transportation, are delivering measurable savings.

evidence.text: Our consumption trends continued to improve due to certain operational initiatives to increase our average miles per gallon as well as the increased use of newer, more fuel-efficient equipment. Purchased transportation expenses decreased to 4.3% of revenue in the first quarter of 2015 from 4.7% of revenue in the first quarter of 2014.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Evidence shows measurable savings from cost reduction initiatives like fuel efficiency.

factors.summary: Operational efficiency is improving, particularly in fuel efficiency and miles per gallon.

evidence.text: Our consumption trends continued to improve due to certain operational initiatives to increase our average miles per gallon as well as the increased use of newer, more fuel-efficient equipment.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Operational efficiency improvements in fuel efficiency and miles per gallon are evident.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is increasing through investments in tractors, trailers, and service centers.

evidence.text: Our capital expenditure plan for 2015 is higher than expenditures in 2014, and consequently we expect depreciation costs to increase in future periods. While our investments can increase our short-term costs, we believe these investments are necessary to support our growth initiatives.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Investments in infrastructure to support growth indicate a positive outlook.

factors.summary: The company is in growth mode, with hiring new drivers and employees to support expansion.

evidence.text: The increases in salaries and wages, excluding benefits, are primarily due to the 17.0% increase in the average number of full-time employees as compared to the first quarter of 2014 and the annual wage increase provided to employees in the third quarter of 2014. The increase in our full-time employees was due primarily to new drivers, platform employees and fleet technicians who were necessary to keep pace with our current and anticipated growth.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Hiring to support growth shows a positive trajectory.

factors.summary: Labor relations are stable, as the company is union-free.

evidence.text: We are a leading, less-than-truckload ('LTL'), union-free motor carrier providing regional, inter-regional and national LTL services...

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Union-free status contributes to stable labor relations.

factors.summary: Management is confident, as evidenced by stock buybacks and capital expenditures.

evidence.text: During the three months ended March 31, 2015, we repurchased 180,638 shares for $13.3 million. As of March 31, 2015, we had repurchased a total of 251,258 shares for $18.9 million, and $181.1 million remained authorized under the program.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Stock buybacks indicate strong management confidence in the company's future.

factors.summary: The company has a clear long-term strategic direction, supported by significant capital expenditures.

evidence.text: Our capital expenditure plan for 2015 is higher than expenditures in 2014, and consequently we expect depreciation costs to increase in future periods. While our investments can increase our short-term costs, we believe these investments are necessary to support our growth initiatives.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Significant capital expenditures support a clear long-term growth strategy.

factors.summary: Fuel prices and economic conditions are key sensitivities impacting the company.

evidence.text: Fuel surcharges are designed to offset fluctuations in the cost of petroleum-based products and are one of the many components included in the overall negotiated price we charge for our services. Freight volumes typically build to a peak in the third or early fourth quarter, which generally results in improved operating margins for those periods.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company manages fuel price risks through surcharges and expects improved margins in peak quarters.

factors.summary: Catalysts for growth include new service center investments and technology upgrades.

evidence.text: Our capital expenditure plan for 2015 is higher than expenditures in 2014, and consequently we expect depreciation costs to increase in future periods. While our investments can increase our short-term costs, we believe these investments are necessary to support our growth initiatives.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Increased capital expenditures for growth initiatives indicate significant investment in future expansion.

factors.summary: The company is gaining market share, particularly in premium services.

evidence.text: We believe the increase in tonnage continues to be driven by further market share gains as the value of our premium service resonates with our customers.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is successfully gaining market share, particularly in premium services, driven by customer value.

factors.summary: The company's competitive advantage lies in its premium service offerings.

evidence.text: We believe the increase in tonnage continues to be driven by further market share gains as the value of our premium service resonates with our customers.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: The company's premium service offerings provide a strong competitive advantage, supported by market share gains.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are increasing to support growth initiatives.

evidence.text: Our capital expenditure plan for 2015 is higher than expenditures in 2014, and consequently we expect depreciation costs to increase in future periods. While our investments can increase our short-term costs, we believe these investments are necessary to support our growth initiatives.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Higher capital expenditures in 2015 compared to 2014 indicate a commitment to growth initiatives, despite short-term costs.

factors.summary: The company is returning capital to shareholders through stock buybacks.

evidence.text: During the three months ended March 31, 2015, we repurchased 180,638 shares for $13.3 million. As of March 31, 2015, we had repurchased a total of 251,258 shares for $18.9 million, and $181.1 million remained authorized under the program.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Share buybacks demonstrate a commitment to returning capital to shareholders, enhancing value.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving, with positive cash flows from operations.

evidence.text: Cash flows provided by operating activities were $172,594 thousand for the first quarter of 2015, an increase of $73,026 thousand compared to the first quarter of 2014.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Significantly increased cash flows from operations indicate strong financial health and growth potential.

factors.summary: The overall economic environment is a tailwind, with demand tied to industrial production and the U.S. domestic economy.

evidence.text: More than 95% of our revenue has historically been derived from transporting LTL shipments for our customers, whose demand for our services is generally tied to industrial production and the overall health of the U.S. domestic economy.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong demand tied to industrial production and the U.S. domestic economy provides a favorable tailwind.

factors.summary: Environmental compliance costs are manageable and not material.

evidence.text: We do not believe that the cost of future compliance with current environmental laws or regulations will have a material adverse effect on our operations, financial condition, competitive position or capital expenditures for the remainder of fiscal year 2015 or fiscal year 2016.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company believes environmental compliance costs will not have a material adverse effect, indicating a positive outlook.
